old_claim.note: Basic Woodworking is an earlier historical predecessor or foundation, not a one-to-one engineering dependency.

new_claim.note: The trireme's light, slender hull depended critically on a carefully built timber structure, so shaping and joining wood was constitutive of the vessel.

source_shape.source_locator: Engineering Colloquium abstract: the extreme slender-hull design relied critically on details of its timber structure and was reconstructed from ancient building evidence.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The NASA-hosted Trireme Trust lecture abstract makes the timber structure critical to the viable lightweight hull design.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: This is a material and fabrication dependency of the vessel itself, not merely an earlier cultural tradition that happened to precede shipbuilding.

ontology_before: Woodworking was represented only as distant prehistory despite forming the trireme's load-bearing hull.
ontology_after: Shaped and joined timber is a required direct prerequisite of the scoped Greek trireme.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed and locked by graph invariant 2026-07-16-greek-trireme-boundary: woodworking_basic remains required.
would_reject_if: A historically supported Greek trireme design uses a non-wooden hull whose structure can be built without shaped or joined timber.
short_reason: The reconstruction evidence identifies the timber structure as critical to the trireme's extreme slender hull.
